steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a subject (‘Her innovative poems’) and a direct object (‘the use of Jamaican Creole in literature’). The blank is the verb ‘popularized’ connecting them. The question asks which version of the verb is punctuated correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — popularized:: A colon cannot separate a transitive verb from its direct object. ✗
  • Choice B — popularized,: A comma cannot separate a transitive verb from its direct object. ✗
  • Choice C — popularized: No punctuation between the verb and its direct object is the correct rule. The verb-object pair must remain uninterrupted. ✓
  • Choice D — popularized;: A semicolon cannot separate a transitive verb from its direct object. ✗
That is the correct answer.
